en: Accents to such a small country, everybody sort of puts on different voices when they're doing little bits.
ko: 그렇게 작은 나라인데, 사람들이 장난칠 때 다양한 억양을 흉내 내죠.

en: Yeah, yeah, but I had a thing where I was checking into a hotel and
ko: 네, 네, 그런데 호텔에 체크인할 때 있었던 일인데,

en: somebody came out and said, "Oh, do you need help with your bags?" And I thought they were doing a bit.
ko: 누군가 나와서 '가방 도와드릴까요?' 하길래 장난하는 줄 알았어요.

en: I went, "Oh, I've been traveling all day." I have no idea...
ko: 그래서 '아, 하루 종일 여행했거든요' 하고 따라 했는데,

en: So I had to like slowly transition out of it.
And I know that that sounds like an exaggeration of their voice,
ko: 그리고 그게 그 사람 목소리를 과장한 것처럼 들린다는 걸 알지만,

en: but then I was back in the country several years later and they were on TV,
ko: 몇 년 후에 다시 그 나라에 갔는데, 그 사람들이 TV에 나오더라고요.

en: on Gogglebox, because it was Steph and Dom from Gogglebox.
So they actually talk like that?
ko: 그래서 진짜 그렇게 말하더라고요. 거기가 진짜 호텔인 줄 몰랐어요. 네, 거기 묵었어요.

en: That is... I never knew that that was a real hotel.
Yeah, and accidentally made fun of them to their face.
ko: 네, 그리고 실수로 그 사람들 앞에서 놀린 거죠.

en: Alan, what's that story you have about that?
You're a very good friend of mine.
She was going through a really stressful time, and a lovely aunt said...
ko: 정말 힘든 시기를 겪고 있었는데, 다정한 이모가 말했어요.
